title: Bearing False Testimony
subtitle: Lying Before Allah's Justice — A Crime That Destroys the Innocent

verses.arabic: وَالَّذِينَ لَا يَشْهَدُونَ الزُّورَ وَإِذَا مَرُّوا بِاللَّغْوِ مَرُّوا كِرَامًا
verses.translation: And [they are] those who do not testify to falsehood, and when they pass near ill speech, they pass by with dignity.

verses.arabic: فَاجْتَنِبُوا الرِّجْسَ مِنَ الْأَوْثَانِ وَاجْتَنِبُوا قَوْلَ الزُّورِ
verses.translation: So avoid the uncleanliness of idols and avoid false speech.

verses.arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُونُوا قَوَّامِينَ بِالْقِسْطِ شُهَدَاءَ لِلَّهِ وَلَوْ عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَوِ الْوَالِدَيْنِ وَالْأَقْرَبِينَ
verses.translation: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm in justice, witnesses for Allah, even if it be against yourselves or parents and relatives.

brief_explanation: The Quran pairs the avoidance of false testimony with idolatry — treating 'qawl al-zur' (false speech/testimony) as a form of filth (rijs) comparable to the filth of idols, underscoring its severity as a spiritual and social corruption. Bearing false witness destroys the entire foundation of justice in a society, leading to wrongful convictions, execution of the innocent, and theft of rights — all compounded on the shoulders of the one who bore false witness. The Quran identifies truthful testimony as an act of worship ('witnesses for Allah') even when it goes against oneself or one's own family, establishing that justice transcends personal loyalty. The Prophet (peace be upon him) ranked false testimony as equal to shirk in its destructiveness.
capital_punishment: There is no separate hadd execution specifically for false testimony. Its legal consequence depends on the harm caused: if false testimony led to an unjust execution, the false witness bears moral and potentially legal culpability for that death. If it led to wrongful lashing, some scholars hold the false witness should receive the equivalent punishment. Scholars of all four madhabs agree that a false witness loses their credibility permanently and is disqualified from future testimony. The state may impose ta'zir (imprisonment, public shaming, lashing) as a deterrent.
